channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Election day confusion in Louisiana after voting changes
item.description: The governor of Louisiana canceled the U.S. House primaries after tens of thousands of votes had already been cast. On Election Day, we hear from voters trying to make sense of the last-minute changes.
item.content:encoded: The governor of Louisiana canceled the U.S. House primaries after tens of thousands of votes had already been cast. On Election Day, we hear from voters trying to make sense of the last-minute changes.
item.dc:creator: Acacia Squires

item.title: Iranian man barely survives bombing, but wants war to go on
item.description: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.
item.content:encoded: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: Understanding modern China through reporting on a hidden family history
item.description: While in China to cover President Trump's visit to meet with leader Xi Jinping, host of NPR and WBUR's Here & Now Scott Tong spent time learning about a difficult chapter in his family's history.
item.content:encoded: While in China to cover President Trump's visit to meet with leader Xi Jinping, host of NPR and WBUR's Here & Now Scott Tong spent time learning about a difficult chapter in his family's history.
item.dc:creator: Scott Tong

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: producing a great interview
item.description: The team behind NPR's Wild Card explains how careful preparation helps them produce interviews that reveal deep and surprising human moments.
item.content:encoded: The team behind NPR's Wild Card explains how careful preparation helps them produce interviews that reveal deep and surprising human moments.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Geiran

item.title: The lasting power of the show 'Survivor'
item.description: Ahead of the Season 50 finale of the beloved CBS show, two time "Survivor" player Rob Cesternino talks to NPR's Emily Feng about what gives the reality competition show such lasting power.
item.content:encoded: Ahead of the Season 50 finale of the beloved CBS show, two time "Survivor" player Rob Cesternino talks to NPR's Emily Feng about what gives the reality competition show such lasting power.
item.dc:creator: Mia Venkat

item.title: Life on the edge: why Indonesians living near volcanoes won't leave
item.description: Indonesia has more than 120 active volcanoes. On the island of Java, thousands live alongside Mount Merapi, constantly balancing risks -- and most say they won't leave.
item.content:encoded: Indonesia has more than 120 active volcanoes. On the island of Java, thousands live alongside Mount Merapi, constantly balancing risks -- and most say they won't leave.
item.dc:creator: Naina Rao

item.title: A surprising solution to cut asphalt's impact on human health
item.description: Elham Fini, professor of Sustainable Engineering and the Built Environment at Arizona State University, talks about her work on the health impact of asphalt emissions and a solution that could minimize the harmful effects.
item.content:encoded: Elham Fini, professor of Sustainable Engineering and the Built Environment at Arizona State University, talks about her work on the health impact of asphalt emissions and a solution that could minimize the harmful effects.
item.dc:creator: Tinbete Ermyas

item.title: Iran has been confiscating property from critics of the regime
item.description: Iran's government has been confiscating property from people it deems traitors or critics of the regime.
item.content:encoded: Iran's government has been confiscating property from people it deems traitors or critics of the regime.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: The White House is planning a big prayer event. Almost all the speakers are Christian
item.description: As the White House prepares for a prayer event tied to the nation's 250th anniversary, some question why the lineup is almost entirely Christian.
item.content:encoded: As the White House prepares for a prayer event tied to the nation's 250th anniversary, some question why the lineup is almost entirely Christian.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: With plummeting support for immigration policies, Trump administration shifts approach
item.description: The Trump administration is shifting their public approach on immigration as Congressional Republicans push to fund ICE through the end of Trump's term.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration is shifting their public approach on immigration as Congressional Republicans push to fund ICE through the end of Trump's term.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo
